text:
is an Evangelion Unit belonging to the Rebuild of Evangelion film series. It appears in the movie Evangelion: 3.0+1.0 Thrice Upon A Time. Mark.09-A is a new development of Evangelion Mark.09, the Master of the AAA Wunder and one of the remaining Adams. Like the other Vessels of the Adams, the Mark.09-A is piloted by the advanced Ayanami Series. 

Profile

Official name:
EVANGELION Mark.09-A

EVANGELION Mark.09-A shares the design of the Vessels of the Adams units with the distinct coloring of yellow and black and one extra lines on the right of its face mask to show the Roman numerals for 9 (IX). Like the other Vessels of the Adams, a small SEELE insignia can be seen on the Mark.09-A's forehead. The mask can also be open and a shape similar to the second head of Mark.09 can be seen being compressed by the mask.

Features

Mark.09-A (Along with the other Vessels of the Adams) does not have a A.T. Field. It has the ability to produce a halo below or behind it to levitate. It also possess the ability to open its masks to fire beams of light.

History

Evangelion: 3.0+1.0 Thrice Upon A Time

Evangelion Mark.09-A aboards the AAA Wunder and it is identified by Ritsuko as Pattern Blue and an Opfer Type Evangelion and just like Evangelion Mark.09 did previously, releases a blue ooze from its hands that pours into the ship interior, hijacking its controls from the crew, overwriting the system and reverting it to NHG Buβe. The bridge is physically lowered and all lockdown systems are deactivated, releasing Shinji from his cell.

It allows Fuyutsuki to take control of the ships and initiate the forced conversion of the Black Moon into the spears and so initiating the Fourth Impact. After that, Mark.09-A is ambushed by Unit-08γ, which reveals it face and proceeds to bite and remove Mark.09-A's right arm and synthesizes it into its own as Mark.09-A struggles. After that its neck is bitten again and devoured by Unit-08γ.

It is last seen as it vanishes from the world, inside the Unit-08γ as part of the Final Evangelion.

Trivia

The yellow decals correspond to those on the AAA Wunder, since it is the master of the airship. This color also corresponds to Famine, one of the Horsemen of the Apocalypse as shown in the by .
